question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user query a topic ID whose record was never exported to the mirror node (e.g., due to importer downtime), causing the mirror node to return EntityNotFoundException while the consensus node has the topic, resulting in incorrect data availability for downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicRepository.findById() queries only the current topic table (not the topic_history table) to observe a topic that was recently updated as missing or stale, causing incorrect state to be exported to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition by querying TopicRepository.findById() during an upsert operation on the topic table, causing the mirror node to export an intermediate or partial topic record with missing fields such as adminKey or submitKey?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null timestampRange due to an incomplete upsert, causing the mirror node to export an incorrect record that misrepresents the topic's active period?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicRepository.findById() uses the numeric Long id (not the full shard.realm.num EntityId) to retrieve a topic, causing cross-shard or cross-realm topic ID collisions that result in incorrect topic records being exported to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a topic ID where the numeric portion matches a deleted topic's id in the database, causing TopicRepository.findById() to return a stale deleted-topic record that was never properly removed from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null feeScheduleKey even though the consensus node has a non-null feeScheduleKey, because the importer failed to export the key update record to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null feeExemptKeyList even though the consensus node has a non-null feeExemptKeyList, because the importer failed to export the key update record to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null adminKey even though the consensus node has a non-null adminKey, because the importer failed to export the adminKey update record to the mirror node, causing incorrect access control information to be served?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null submitKey even though the consensus node has a non-null submitKey, because the importer failed to export the submitKey update record to the mirror node, causing incorrect submit permission information to be served?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null createdTimestamp because the topic creation record was missing from the stream file exported to the mirror node, causing incorrect historical data to be served?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Upsertable(history = true) annotation on AbstractTopic to observe that a topic update was written to the history table but not promoted to the current topic table, causing TopicRepository.findById() to return an outdated record that does not reflect the latest consensus state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic whose timestampRange upper bound is incorrectly set (topic appears deleted/expired) due to a missing or incorrect record in the mirror node export, causing the topic to appear inactive when it is still active on the consensus node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic whose timestampRange lower bound is incorrect due to a missing creation record in the mirror node export, causing incorrect historical data to be served to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with incorrect adminKey bytes due to a partial or truncated record being exported to the mirror node, causing consumers to make incorrect access control decisions based on the wrong key?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with incorrect submitKey bytes due to a partial or truncated record being exported to the mirror node, causing consumers to make incorrect submit permission decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with incorrect feeScheduleKey bytes due to a partial or truncated record being exported to the mirror node, causing consumers to make incorrect fee schedule decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with incorrect feeExemptKeyList bytes due to a partial or truncated record being exported to the mirror node, causing consumers to make incorrect fee exemption decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic record that was never deleted from the mirror node database even though the topic was deleted on the consensus node, because the deletion record was missing from the exported stream file?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic record that is missing from the mirror node database even though the topic exists on the consensus node, because the creation record was missing from the exported stream file, causing EntityNotFoundException to be thrown incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a stale adminKey from a previous update because the latest adminKey update record was missing from the mirror node export, causing consumers to use an outdated key?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a stale submitKey from a previous update because the latest submitKey update record was missing from the mirror node export, causing consumers to use an outdated key?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a stale feeScheduleKey from a previous update because the latest feeScheduleKey update record was missing from the mirror node export, causing consumers to use an outdated fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a stale feeExemptKeyList from a previous update because the latest feeExemptKeyList update record was missing from the mirror node export, causing consumers to use an outdated fee exemption list?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with a null id field due to a database integrity issue caused by a missing or malformed record in the mirror node export, causing incorrect data to be served?
